more_vertModel Thoughts Defining the OutcomeI'm currently focused on defining the exact output structure. I will be extracting structured information from YouTube video transcripts and need to ensure the final result is a valid JSON object. I'm prioritizing generating a concise summary, ideally 2-3 sentences long, as part of the desired JSON format.Outlining the StructureMy focus has shifted to the proposed structure for the output JSON. I'm currently fleshing out the 'keyPoints' section, which will include lists for 'aiConcepts,' 'technicalDetails,' and 'visualContextFlags.' I am excited about the implementation plan that is underway. The "LLM Selector" should be complete shortly!Developing the RouterI'm currently working on the "selector" LLM aspect, specifically focusing on Neural Chat, which will read descriptions and select a model name. I'm building a command-line and Streamlit UI. The system should intelligently route queries, for example, "basic math" to Neural Chat or "Python code" to Code Llama. My goal is to add memory and host the setup, which will support more models and eventually fine-tuned models.Building the InterfacesI've been concentrating on the interfaces, specifically a terminal script and a Streamlit web UI, for the LLM selector. Neural Chat is the chosen LLM for strict model name outputs. My system is structured around AI concepts like LLMs, prompt engineering, and zero-shot classification. I am incorporating technical details, including Ollama, LangChain, and Python 3.11. I am including Visual Context Flags by using timestamps from example videos.Showcasing the InterfaceI am now focusing on the Streamlit UI demonstration, showcasing the web application. I've designed the UI to highlight the dynamic routing capabilities, for tasks such as "coding," "general chat," and "uncensored queries". I'm looking to highlight the local LLM selection system that uses Ollama and LangChain, which automatically routes user queries to the most appropriate installed model.Finalizing the OutputI've been working on a streamlined JSON structure with no markdown or extraneous text. My goal is a valid JSON output that directly incorporates the "visualContextFlags" using timestamps and descriptions for enhanced clarity. I've ensured concise summaries that directly address the specific requirements outlined in the instructions. Expand to view model thoughts  chevron_right

editmore_vert{"summary": "This video introduces a local LLM selection system built with Ollama and LangChain that automatically routes user queries to the most appropriate installed model. By employing a 'selector' LLM to evaluate model descriptions, it eliminates the manual effort of switching between specialized tools like Code Llama or Mistral.","keyPoints": ["Automating the selection of local LLMs based on task requirements to avoid manual switching","Using a 'selector' LLM (Neural Chat) to evaluate query context against a dictionary of available models","Implementing a modular system that supports coding, general chat, and uncensored tasks","Providing both a terminal-based implementation and a Streamlit web UI","Integrating Ollama for efficient local model management and rapid loading"],"aiConcepts": ["LLM Routing","Model Orchestration","Local Inference","Agentic Workflows","Zero-shot Classification","Prompt Engineering"],"technicalDetails": ["Ollama","LangChain","Streamlit","Python 3.11","Conda environment management","Neural Chat model","Code Llama model","Llama 2 Uncensored model","GitHub (promptingjunior48/llm-selector)"],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 270,"reason": "Repository Structure","context": "Displays the GitHub repository 'llm-selector' and explain the file manifest"},{"timestamp": 360,"reason": "Logic explanation","context": "Shows VS Code and explains the model dictionary mapping names to specific descriptions"},{"timestamp": 720,"reason": "Interface Demo","context": "Demonstration of the Streamlit web UI showing automatic model switching in the sidebar"}]}thumb_upthumb_down
